contaminant.name: Automotive Road Grime
contaminant.description: Road-grime contamination builds up on vehicle surfaces during everyday driving, as dirt particles mix with oil residues and salt from winter roads or coastal sprays, creating a stubborn layered film that clings tightly unlike simpler dust or rust alone. This mix sets in differently on steel, where it accelerates corrosion through salt's moisture-trapping action, versus aluminum, which resists deeper pitting but still faces adhesive oil buildup that dulls finishes over time. Laser cleaning tackles these challenges effectively by vaporizing the grime layer without damaging underlying metals, ramping up removal speed while cutting down on chemical use for a clean, efficient process.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
